title: Bot mode for difficulty validation and auto-pilot watching
description:
## Context

The wave difficulty curve is currently tuned so the announced `target_wpm` of each wave matches the survival floor (wave 1 = 20 wpm clears 10 zombies in 36 s, etc.). There's no way to verify this contract visually — we have to trust the math. We also want a satisfying "auto-pilot" demo mode that anyone can watch.

## Goal

Add an in-game **bot mode** that simulates a typist matching the current wave's announced `target_wpm`. The bot replaces keyboard input and shows whether the wave is actually beatable at the announced WPM. Doubles as a regression check whenever wave balance is tweaked.

## Expected behavior

- **Activation.** A toggle reachable from the main menu (e.g. a new "BOT" entry alongside SURVIVAL/ZEN/SOUND) and a keyboard shortcut while playing (e.g. F2) that turns the bot on/off mid-session.
- **Typing simulation.** While the bot is active it produces synthetic keypresses into the same input buffer the player uses. The cadence is fixed by the active wave: chars/sec = `target_wpm / 12` (standard 5-chars-per-word convention).
- **Reaction time.** The bot is *not* instantaneous. It has a configurable reaction delay (default ~200 ms) before it commits to a new target zombie or switches targets when the situation changes. Without this handicap the bot would survive at WPM tiers a human couldn't — defeating the validation purpose.
- **Target selection.** Each tick the bot picks the most-pressing zombie (e.g. the one closest to the bottom). If its current target dies or is killed by collateral, it picks a new one after the reaction delay.
- **Visual indicator.** While the bot is active the HUD shows an unambiguous "BOT" badge (top-center or near the WPM/accuracy stats). Color/style should make it clear the player is not currently controlling typing.
- **No high-score persistence.** Sessions played in bot mode never write to `highscore.dat` (or the localStorage equivalent on web). The best-score display can still update in-memory for the current run but must not survive a restart.
- **No power-up usage.** Carrier zombies still spawn power-ups (visual variety), but the bot never activates or consumes any held power-up (no Space/Tab/whatever shortcut). Picked-up power-ups simply sit in the inventory until the wave ends.
- **Mid-session toggle.** Turning the bot on/off during a wave is allowed. The moment a player takes manual control mid-bot-session, the high-score gate stays disabled for the remainder of that session (you can't "cheese" by letting the bot carry you most of the way).
- **Game-over and waves.** Otherwise behaves exactly like survival: dying state, wave transitions, boss waves all apply. The bot must also be able to type boss phrases.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Bot mode can be entered from the main menu and toggled with a keyboard shortcut while playing.
- [ ] A visual "BOT" indicator is visible whenever bot mode is active.
- [ ] At wave 1 with the default `target_wpm = 20` and the default reaction delay, the bot clears the wave with no zombies landing (validates the survival-floor contract).
- [ ] No write to `highscore.dat` or the web localStorage equivalent occurs for any session that touched bot mode.
- [ ] No power-up is consumed by the bot, regardless of pickups during the run.
- [ ] Boss phrases are typed correctly by the bot.
- [ ] Reaction delay is a tunable constant (so we can sweep "bot at 20 wpm with 0/100/200/500 ms reaction" if needed for balance research).
- [ ] Manual control during a bot session disables the high-score gate for the rest of that run.

## Out of scope

- Typo simulation / error rate (can be a follow-up if the bot turns out to be "too clean" — first iteration is perfect-keystrokes).
- Multi-bot or competitive bot vs. player.
- Network-mode automated balancing harness (this is an in-game observability tool, not a CI tool).
